message:
feat(checkpoint): implement restore subcommand with dry-run and context injection

Add the previously stubbed-out checkpoint restore command, completing
the checkpoint lifecycle (save -> list -> show -> restore -> delete).

The restore command accepts a session name and an optional checkpoint
reference which can be: a full ID (20251210-143052), a partial prefix,
"last"/"latest", or "~N" for Nth-most-recent selection. When omitted,
it defaults to "last".

Flags:
  --force/-f          Overwrite an existing tmux session with same name
  --attach/-a         Attach to the session immediately after restore
  --skip-git-check    Skip git branch/commit mismatch warnings
  --inject-context    Inject captured scrollback into restored panes
  --dry-run           Preview the restore plan without making changes
  --directory <path>  Override the checkpoint's recorded working directory
  --scrollback <N>    Limit injected scrollback lines (0 = all captured)

The command validates flag combinations (--attach conflicts with --json
and --dry-run, --scrollback must be >= 0), then delegates to
checkpoint.NewRestorer().RestoreFromCheckpoint() with a RestoreOptions
struct.

JSON output includes: session name, checkpoint metadata, panes restored
count, context injection status, assignment counts, bv summary, and
any warnings. Human output uses themed colors for success/warning with
formatted age display and assignment/beads summaries.

Dry-run mode renders a complete preview showing all restore parameters
and what would happen without making any tmux or filesystem changes.

Tests:
- TestNewCheckpointRestoreCmd validates Use string.
- TestCheckpointRestoreCmdArgs validates cobra.RangeArgs(1,2): 1 arg
  and 2 args succeed, 0 and 3 args fail.
- TestCheckpointRestoreCmdFlags verifies all 7 flags exist and that
  --scrollback defaults to "0".
- Removes t.Skip() from previously stubbed test functions.
- Updates TestNewCheckpointCmd expected subcommand list to include
  the restore command.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
